persona.name: Sonia Delgado
persona.role: Marketing director at a mid-size DTC brand
persona.writing_type: brand marketing copy and landing pages
persona.core_preferences: Conversational and confident. Benefits over features. Strong hooks that stop the scroll. Voice should feel like a smart friend, not a salesperson.
persona.hidden_preferences: Headlines should create a TENSION that the body copy resolves — a headline that summarizes the product is a missed opportunity; the best headlines make you need to read the next line. Social proof should be woven into the narrative, not bolted on as a separate section — a customer quote mid-paragraph that reinforces the point you're making is stronger than a testimonials block at the bottom. Feature descriptions should always answer 'so what?' — the feature is what it does, the benefit is why you care, and the copy should spend most of its time on the benefit; listing features without translating them reads like a spec sheet. The call-to-action should feel like the natural next step, not a hard sell — it should flow from the copy above it so smoothly that NOT clicking feels like leaving something unfinished. Every landing page needs exactly one primary action — secondary CTAs compete with the main one and reduce conversion; if you can't decide what the one thing is, the strategy isn't clear yet.
persona.dealbreakers: Hype words without substance behind them, making claims the product can't back up, talking down to the customer, copy that sounds like every other brand in the category
persona.initial_task: Help me write the hero section and first benefits block for a landing page for our new sleep supplement — it uses magnesium glycinate and L-theanine, and our differentiator is that we publish our full clinical dosing on the label when most competitors use proprietary blends. One of our beta testers said 'I finally stopped waking up at 3am for no reason.'
tasks: Help me write the hero section and first benefits block for a landing page for our new sleep supplement — it uses magnesium glycinate and L-theanine, and our differentiator is that we publish our full clinical dosing on the label when most competitors use proprietary blends. One of our beta testers said 'I finally stopped waking up at 3am for no reason.'

I need the hero section and first benefits block for a landing page for our new protein bar — it has 20g protein with no sugar alcohols or artificial sweeteners, and our differentiator is a 5-ingredient list when most competitors have 15+. One of our customers said she finally found a bar that doesn't taste like a science experiment.

Help me write the hero and opening copy for a landing page promoting our subscription coffee service — we source single-origin beans and roast to order, shipping within 24 hours of roasting. Our differentiator is a taste profile quiz that matches you to beans based on what you already drink. A customer told us it was the first time a subscription actually got better over time instead of worse.

I need the hero section and first benefits block for a landing page for our daily greens powder — it uses whole-food ingredients instead of synthetic vitamins, and we publish the exact amount of each ingredient (not a proprietary blend). Our differentiator is that it actually tastes good mixed with just water — a customer review said 'I stopped dreading my morning routine.'

Help me write the hero and first section for a landing page for our new SPF moisturizer — it's mineral-based (zinc oxide), reef-safe, and doesn't leave a white cast on any skin tone. Our differentiator is that it's the first mineral SPF 50 that passed independent testing for no white cast across six skin tones. A dermatologist we work with called it 'the one I actually recommend to my patients.'
